3d-asset.title: 3D asset
3d-asset.caption: A 3D prop: concept → Blender blockout → export → validated Pattern-B import.

steps.label: Concept / production board
inputs: A prop brief — silhouette · scale · domain

steps.label: Blockout in Blender
steps.note: Greybox the form at correct scale before detailing.
substeps: Greybox the form at correct scale

refs.title: Reference the shared asset library via external-locations

audio-production.title: Audio production
audio-production.caption: A briefed audio cue, generated → reviewed → filed into its target slot (Pattern A).

steps.label: Write the audio brief
steps.note: The target slot is the leaf's parameter point: library audio/<context>/ by default; a calling workflow (e.g. character-creation's audio-identity) may bind a by-reference destination.

steps.label: Stage to _inbox/audio/
steps.note: _inbox/ is the only place machine output lands, transient by contract (ADR 0008). Triage moves keeps onward; drops die here.

steps.label: Audition takes
steps.note: Approve a take, or regenerate.

steps.label: Place per the brief's target slot
steps.detail: library audio/<context>/ — or a bound destination

refs.title: Reference the shared asset library via external-locations

background-music-audio.title: Background music audio
background-music-audio.caption: <one line: what this process reliably produces>

steps.label: <step>
steps.note: <shown in the inspector on click>

character-creation.title: Character creation
character-creation.caption: A lore-locked character: six-stage visual ladder + optional audio identity + 3D handoff, one pipeline per character.

steps.label: Lore lock
steps.note: The lore-brain engram is the source of truth. Before any art: confirm it carries visual tells (palette, silhouette, materials, one signature detail). If it doesn't, run lore-creation to add them there first — briefs quote the engram, never invent.

steps.label: Concept (_concept-v1)
steps.note: One image-generation run with templates/character/concept.svg as composition ref. Goal: the character reads at a glance — silhouette, palette, attitude.

steps.label: Portrait (_portrait-v1)
steps.note: One image-generation run, templates/character/portrait.svg as composition ref. 2:3 bust, eye-line on the upper-third guide. The only stage where an in-world atmospheric background is expected.

steps.label: Model sheet (front · side-back)
steps.note: One image-generation run, templates/character/turnaround.svg as composition ref. Neutral studio-gray, relaxed A-pose, same scale across views, shared ground line. This pair is the primary 3D spec.

steps.label: Detail passes (props · materials)
steps.note: One image-generation run per signature item, templates/character/detail-callouts.svg as composition ref: hero panels + close-up column + material swatch row (the Penitent Knight helm-sheet layout).

steps.label: Pose sheet (action + personality)
steps.note: One image-generation run, templates/character/pose-sheet.svg as composition ref. Action and personality poses on neutral background — silhouette-check every cell.

steps.label: Set review — roster consistency
steps.note: Gate: the set coheres with the templates AND the roster — crops match, palette matches the engram, and it reads as the same being in every deliverable.

steps.label: File to lore-brain Media/visual
steps.note: File under the grammar, then link the set back on the persona engram so the brain knows its own face.

steps.label: Audio identity (optional)
steps.note: Theme song / voice sample via the audio-production workflow. Optional lane — skip without blocking the 3D handoff.

steps.label: 3D handoff → 3d-asset
steps.note: Seed a 3d-asset run: the turnaround pair + detail sheets are the modeling spec; poses inform rig checks.

image-generation.title: Image generation
image-generation.caption: A briefed 2D image: batch-generated by Codex → reviewed → finalized into its target slot (Pattern A).

steps.label: Write the image brief
steps.note: The target slot is the leaf's parameter point: library ui/ | textures/ by default; a calling workflow (e.g. character-creation) may bind a template as composition ref, a filename grammar, and a by-reference destination.

steps.label: Write generation/BATCH.md
steps.note: One rolling contract, rewritten per batch. Composition refs (e.g. templates/character/ PNG twins) named in the batch or staged under generation/refs/; if the tool can't take image inputs, the template's constraints are encoded textually in every prompt.

steps.label: Run generation batch <id>
steps.note: The operator trigger phrase. Codex stops if BATCH.md's id doesn't match.

steps.label: Generate into generation/staging/
steps.note: Codex generates candidates only — no review, rename, or filing on the Codex side. Staging is untracked; candidates are ephemeral by design.

steps.label: Review candidates vs brief + template
steps.note: Claude reviews against the brief and composition ref and shortlists keeps; the operator has final say. Approve one, or send back for another pass.

steps.label: Refine the brief · rewrite the batch
steps.note: Tighten the prompt and regenerate.

steps.label: Place per the brief's target slot
steps.detail: library ui/ | textures/ — or a bound destination

refs.title: Reference the shared asset library via external-locations

lore-creation.title: Lore creation
lore-creation.caption: A lore deliverable — engram · brief · shippable text — authored into the lore-brain, by reference only.

steps.label: Write the lore brief
steps.note: Three deliverable classes per the medium card: engram (canon source) · brief (what downstream workflows consume) · shippable text (dialog, flavor, lyrics).

steps.label: Continuity pass (/lore-brain:recall)
steps.note: Quality bar: no contradictions with established attributes; scars surfaced before authoring. Briefs quote the graph, never invent.

steps.label: Author / generate
steps.note: Content matches the reality's declared register (soul-steel cyber-gothic · tessera process-philosophy · claude-lab speculative-fiction).

steps.label: Canon review
steps.note: Tone matches the register; canon_status set honestly (speculative stays speculative until promoted). Approve, or send back.

steps.label: Tighten the brief
steps.note: Re-run the continuity pass if the revision touches established attributes.

steps.label: Reciprocal link to the reality cortex
Lore-lock gate (asset-bound personas)
steps.note: Only for asset-bound personas/regions: the engram must carry visual tells before any downstream medium starts. This gate is what character-creation's lore-lock step calls. Skip for lore that never becomes an asset.

roblox-textured-ui-ux.title: Roblox textured UI/UX
roblox-textured-ui-ux.caption: A briefed Roblox UI surface: render strategy → image-generation call → local Studio proof → approved upload handoff.

steps.label: Lock UX + visual brief
steps.note: Name the UI surface, visual direction, interaction states, target viewports, input modes, localizable text, and acceptance evidence. Approval authorizes local work through Studio validation; final promotion remains a separate gate.

steps.label: Choose native vs image-backed layers
steps.note: Use native frames/text/layout for structure and semantics. Record why each image-backed layer needs ImageLabel or ImageButton and lock ScaleType, ResampleMode, SliceCenter/SliceScale, TileSize, or atlas cell geometry before generation.

steps.label: Write the art contract
steps.note: Bind the call: composition reference = annotated component/specimen layout; filename grammar = lower-kebab + _BLK.png; destination = shared Assets ui/<slot>/ (or ui/<project>/<bundle>/ when project-specific). Keep text out of pixels and define all hover/pressed/disabled variants.

steps.label: Call image-generation
steps.note: Run image-generation with the bound composition reference, filename grammar, and ui/ target slot. The leaf owns its generation/BATCH.md and staging/review loop; this pipeline tracks the accepted art set as this step's outcome.

steps.label: Preflight the image set
steps.note: Verify PNG/alpha where needed, <=4096 per axis, <=20 MB, exact atlas cell geometry, seamless tile edges, 9-slice corner/edge integrity, tint-safe white regions, and absence of embedded localizable text.

steps.label: File the _BLK assets
steps.note: Asset-Studio never stores the payload. Route shared UI art to ui/icons, ui/frames, ui/sprites, ui/backgrounds, or ui/design; route project-presupposing art below ui/<project>/. Generated uncertain drops may pause in _inbox/images, but reviewed work moves into ui/.

refs.title: Reference the shared asset library via external-locations

steps.label: Build the Studio proof
steps.note: In Edit mode, construct a real ScreenGui/component using rbxasset://trembus paths. Preserve the generating Luau or component source and save an .rbxm reference when durability is required.

steps.label: Wire states + load behavior
steps.note: Use ImageButton for interactive image controls, or layer a semantic button over non-interactive art. Exercise hover/press/disabled states, guard initial loading with IsLoaded or ContentProvider:PreloadAsync, and ensure a missing image fails legibly.

steps.label: Device + input + locale QA
steps.note: Use Studio emulation to test smallest/largest supported sizes, landscape/portrait where applicable, mouse/touch/controller focus, long/localized strings, safe areas, and the smallest 9-slice control. Text must remain readable and borders must not overlap content.

steps.label: Visual + performance QA
steps.note: Inspect at native and scaled sizes, validate Fit/Crop/Tile/Slice behavior, reject compression artifacts or one-pixel details that collapse, avoid frequently rebuilding UIGradient sequences, and never tween UIStroke.Thickness on text.

steps.label: Revise the narrowest failed layer
steps.note: Route image defects back to generate-art; route property/layout/state defects back to build-studio-proof. Record which acceptance check failed before revising.

steps.label: Approve final UI set
steps.note: The owner reviews the actual Studio proof and QA evidence. Approval promotes local art from _BLK to _FNL and authorizes the publish handoff; rejection follows the revise branch.

steps.label: Promote _BLK → _FNL
steps.note: Preserve source files and update every local reference. _FNL means upload-ready, not shipped.

steps.label: Upload + clear moderation
steps.note: Upload under the intended experience owner/group, wait for moderation, and record the resulting IDs. A pending or rejected image is not shippable.

steps.label: Swap to rbxassetid:// + recheck
steps.note: Replace Studio-only rbxasset://trembus references in the shipping consumer, centralize IDs when reused, rerun the Studio/device checks against uploaded assets, and capture the final evidence in the pipeline run.

runs.label: First pass — 2 of 5 props
runs.note: Blockouts landed for the first two props; modeling in progress.

runs.label: Recommended pilot — local Studio acceptance
runs.note: Generation, _BLK filing, Studio structure, interaction wiring, and responsive QA are complete. Rendered-asset acceptance is paused because the Studio content symlink rbxasset://trembus is missing after an app update; _FNL promotion and upload remain unauthorized.

runs.label: Lore lock landed — Visual Identity on the engram
runs.trigger: lore medium quality bar, first exercise
runs.note: Continuity pass across Grid District / Roguex Depot / Ghost in the Grid / Monkey-Bot, then a Visual Identity section canonized on the engram: gunmetal chrome + warm amber neon (never gold — that's the tithe's mark), merchant-first silhouette with the Monkey-Bot perch plate, mixed-run materials, and three tells (amber-flicker vocoder grille, authenticator loupe, eleven uncounted copper pins). Visual specs speculative until set-review promotes them. Concept stage is next.

runs.label: Retro-credit — audio identity landed before the workflow existed
runs.note: Ghost in the Grid v1 + 80s v2 predate this pipeline; credited to the audio-identity step. Visual ladder not started — lore-lock is the active step.

runs.label: Introduction authored (lore-creation)
runs.note: Brief = this pipeline's Context (form, beat outline, exclusion checklist). Lore-lock gate skipped: shippable text, not an asset-bound persona.

outputs.label: Concepts/Soul Steel — The Watch Beyond the Wall (Introduction).md (lore-brain)

runs.label: Two narration takes (audio-production)
runs.trigger: Follows run-1 canon review + narrator voices existing
runs.note: eleven_v3, full 495-word text verbatim, no audio tags. Ashael take via the ElevenLabs Player MCP (transport timed out; render recovered from account history — billed once). Custodian take via the web app produced two generations from one render; operator kept both (two-mix precedent; canonical cut = operator pick, open). stage-out deviation: browser downloads moved straight to lore-brain Media/audio/ — _inbox untouched; validate-ids skipped (nothing in the shared library). sensory_assets wired on the intro engram + both narrator engrams.

runs.label: Narrator personas authored (lore-creation)
runs.note: Brief = this pipeline's Context. Lore-lock gate skipped: audio-bound personas, no visual tell yet — a future portrait run reopens it.

runs.label: Voice design + permanent voices (audio-production)
runs.note: Generated via elevenlabs.io Voice Design in the operator's Chrome (connected MCPs cannot create voices); operator auditioned 3 previews per voice audibly and picked (Ashael: take 3; Custodian: take 2). stage-out deviation: previews/takes arrived via browser download, moved straight to the bound destination (lore-brain Media/audio/) — _inbox untouched. validate-ids skipped: nothing placed in the shared Assets library. Registry convention created: mediums/audio § Named voices.

outputs.label: ElevenLabs voice ruxSNzODa3KpaHd5XW6j — Ashael - Soul Steel
ElevenLabs voice VSiMhzoGYAWmh67ySiCN — The Custodian of the Ledger - Soul Steel

character-factory-lore-medium.label: Distill the visual ladder
character-factory-lore-medium.detail: The six-stage ladder (lore-lock → concept → portrait → model sheet → detail passes → poses) extracted from the two characters who ran it ad hoc (Penitent Knight, Raspmutant) into the character-creation composite workflow — each visual stage one image-generation run with a stage template as composition ref, plus an optional audio-identity lane and a 3D handoff. Landed 2026-07-04; the workflow stays status:draft pending the pilot.

character-factory-lore-medium.label: Lore as the fourth medium
character-factory-lore-medium.detail: mediums/lore.md declared — the upstream, by-reference medium (engrams · briefs · shippable text); the lore-brain graph is its library, decision-0001's treaty in its second application. Feeds the character ladder at the lore-lock gate. Status experimental.

character-factory-lore-medium.label: Stage templates
character-factory-lore-medium.detail: templates/character/ SVGs (concept · portrait · turnaround · detail-callouts · pose-sheet) seeded from the Penitent Knight sheets + Raspmutant turnaround; each template is its stage's definition-of-done. png/ twins for attaching as composition refs.

character-factory-lore-medium.label: Pilot — roguex-33
character-factory-lore-medium.detail: Exercise the whole ladder on the brain's most lopsided asset gap via the roguex-33-character pipeline. Audio identity retro-credited done; lore-lock landed 2026-07-06 (a Visual Identity section canonized on the engram); concept stage active. Proves the tracking model (retro-credit, optional lanes) + the templates before the workflow graduates.

character-factory-lore-medium.label: Set-review + graduate the workflow
character-factory-lore-medium.detail: Pass the pilot's set-review gate (roster consistency — reads as the same being across every deliverable), lock the conventions the pilot surfaces, and promote character-creation from draft → active. Seed the roguex-33 3D handoff (turnaround + detail sheets as the model spec).

character-factory-lore-medium.label: Ratchet lore experimental → supported
character-factory-lore-medium.detail: Once lore has been exercised across enough personas/realities to trust the quality bar (continuity pass · tone-match · honest canon_status · lore-lock discipline), promote mediums/lore.md from experimental → supported.

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Audit & taxonomy decision
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: Explorer audited and the target taxonomy chosen — Medium ▸ MediumType adopted, the Soul-Steel domain axis dropped (ADR 0002).

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Asset-registry contract
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: tools/build-asset-registry.mjs (zero-dep) scans external-locations/assets → previews/dashboards/asset-registry.json: 598 records (was 769 pre-restructure — ADR 0008), every existing metadata field preserved.

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Taxonomy refactor
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: Deterministic medium/mediumType derivation in the scanner (leans on the TGL filename grammar); domain kept as a tag only; 0 unknown. Confirmed by a 3-way adversarial verify.

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Explorer view in the app
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: AssetExplorer.tsx + registry.ts in apps/command-center: summary stats + donut, medium/mediumType/status/ext/search filters, grouped card grid, detail inspector. Built on @trembus/ui; typecheck + build clean; adversarially verified.

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Wire it Live
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: The Command Center now builds as a self-contained SPA into previews/app/ (relative base; all contracts inlined); previews/index.html reworked to five Live lens cards incl. a deep-linked Asset Explorer (app/#explorer); the orphaned static command-center.html retired. Verified served from the :4317 static site.

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Preview parity (CF-1)
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: Real previews landed: builder bakes 320px thumbs (sips → previews/thumbs, now untracked/dev-local — ADR 0007) + a served /_assets src; a Vite dev middleware + committed previews/_assets symlink serve /_assets & /thumbs identically on :5175 dev and :4317 static; MediaFrame shows image thumbs (tile) + full image + glb/gltf turntable, AudioWaveform plays audio in the inspector; reveal-in-Finder (POST /api/reveal, Assets-root guarded) with copy-abs fallback + copy-path/copy-id. ADRs 0004–0007. Verified live on dev + static, 0 console errors.

migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.label: Retire the source artifact
migrate-asset-explorer-into-the-command-.detail: ARCHIVED (2026-07-11) — the migrated Explorer fully replaces the Soul-Steel asset-explorer.html, so this milestone's goal is met. Deprecating the *source* generator (tools/build-asset-explorer.mjs) is a cross-repo change deliberately not pursued from Asset-Studio; parked. If ever revisited, the deprecation banner goes in that generator.

root.label: Asset-studio
root.brief: A first-principles, project-agnostic framework for the planning layer of any project. One contract — ProjectEntity — that every planning artifact derives from, plus the zero-dependency tooling that validates, scaffolds, guards, and renders it. This guide is itself derived from the contract: the framework anatomy below is authored, the _project/ surface and conventions are generated live from this project's config, so the guide can't drift from reality.

children.label: .project-system/ — the vendored core
children.brief: How a consuming project carries the framework: vendor schema/ + lib/ + tools/ verbatim into a reserved .project-system/ at the project root, and never edit or rename a file inside it — updating means re-copying the folder. The only project-specific file an adopter writes is project-system.config.json at the root. This core is universal: identical in every project.

children.label: schema/
children.brief: The contract layer — machine-readable JSON Schema. Holds the universal base contract every consumer mirrors, and the meta-schema that validates a project's config.

children.label: project-entity.base.schema.json
children.brief: The universal contract: the three primitives plus the link/rel definitions. Identical across every consuming project — the artifact a consumer mirrors, and what the drift check compares against.

children.label: project-config.schema.json
children.brief: The meta-schema: validates a project's project-system.config.json. The only project-specific file an adopter authors is checked against this.

children.label: lib/
children.brief: The shared library every engine reads through — a zero-dependency parser plus the seam that composes the base schema with a project's config. Single source: no tool re-implements either.

children.label: md.mjs
children.brief: Zero-dependency frontmatter/markdown parser — the single source all tools read frontmatter and body sections through, so they can never drift on what a file says.

children.label: contract.mjs
children.brief: The seam between the project-agnostic engines and a project's specifics. loadContract() composes base schema + config into the runtime `ctx` every engine consumes; loadEntities() reads the _project/ tree, deriving kind from the folder and id from the filename.

children.label: tools/
children.brief: The zero-dependency engines, all reading the one contract via lib/contract.mjs — no check is re-implemented in a second place. The validator is the single check the others reuse.

children.label: validate.mjs
children.brief: The validator + validateEntity — the single check (per-kind enums, link resolution, prose ↔ frontmatter) that the scaffolder, guard, and renderer all reuse.

children.label: new-entity.mjs
children.brief: The scaffolder behind the single /new <kind> command. Validates the kind against your config and writes valid frontmatter + the kind's scaffold sections, so there are no per-kind commands to hand-maintain.

children.label: guard.mjs
children.brief: The PreToolUse guard — the only hook that can block. Rejects any _project/ write that would break the contract (exit 2 + reason); read-only and fails open.

children.label: render-hub.mjs
children.brief: The renderer. Projects the validated entity graph into the two JSON contracts the Command Center consumes (graph.json + hub.json) — including this very guide. Domain-neutral end to end.

children.label: check-consumer-drift.mjs
children.brief: The packaging discipline: asserts each consumer mirrors the canonical contract (schema, validator behavior, and hook parity). The gate that keeps the un-published mirror honest.

children.label: the two hooks
children.brief: The contract is enforced by exactly two Claude Code hooks: a BLOCKING PreToolUse(Write|Edit) guard (tools/guard.mjs — the only hook that can block; exit 2 + reason, read-only, fails open) and an ADVISORY SessionStart health summary (validate.mjs --summary, always exit 0). Rendering is NOT a hook — it's the Command Center's Vite dev plugin. Consumers vendor these verbatim.

children.label: _project/ — planning surface
children.brief: The project's planning surface — every file here is exactly one ProjectEntity. There is one subfolder per declared kind, and the subtree below is generated from THIS project's project-system.config.json, so it reflects exactly the kinds, folders, naming schemes, and status enums this project declares. kind is derived from the folder; id from the filename; the authored surface of a file is just title · status · updated (+ optional links · tags).

children.label: decisions/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "decision". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the serial · 4-digit scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: 0001-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a decision (synthesized from the serial · 4-digit scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: reports/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "report". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "draft" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: 2026-07-12-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a report (synthesized from the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: pipeline/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "pipeline". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "design" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a pipeline (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: roadmap/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "roadmap". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a roadmap (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: 2026-07-12-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a session (synthesized from the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.brief: Holds entities of kind "workflow". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "draft" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a workflow (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: mediums/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "medium". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "experimental" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a medium (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: The three primitives
children.brief: Everything reduces to three primitives — Identity (kind · id · title) · State (status · updated) · Relation (links[]). kind and id are loader-derived from the file's path; the rest is the authored frontmatter. Everything else a file might carry is an optional `tags` facet. A good reduction deletes fields; this one dropped many per-kind interfaces to one shape.

children.label: Relation vocabulary
children.brief: The universal vocabulary for the typed edges between entities (links[].rel), defined once in the base schema and shared by every project. WHICH kinds each rel may point at is this project's config (relTargetKinds) — the facts below are that resolution. A link target is a `<folder>/<id>` path, a milestone marker, or an external ref.
